Home > Rugby World Cup 2027

Rugby World Cup 2027 Guide

The Autumn International rugby matches will give way to something altogether bigger in 2027, when the Rugby World Cup takes place down under in Australia.

England, Ireland, Scotland and Wales will join 20 other nations as they compete for the ultimate prize in rugby – the Webb Ellis Cup – which was last won by the Springboks in 2023.

The opening match of Rugby World Cup 2027 gets underway at Perth Stadium on Friday 1st October. The final will take place six weeks later at Stadium Australia in Sydney on Saturday 13th November.

Rugby World Cup 2027 Fixtures

All kick-off times are shown in UK / Ireland time with local time shown in brackets.

Pool A | Pool B | Pool C | Pool D | Pool E | Pool F | Round of 16 | Quarter Finals | Semi Finals | Bronze Final | Final

Pool A

Friday 1st October 2027

Australia v Hong Kong Perth Stadium, Perth Kick-off: 11:45am (6:45pm local)

Saturday 2nd October 2027

New Zealand v Chile Perth Stadium, Perth Kick-off: 6:15am (1:15pm local)

Saturday 9th October 2027

New Zealand v Australia Stadium Australia, Sydney Kick-off: 7:10am (5:10pm local)
Chile v Hong Kong North Queensland Stadium, Townsville Kick-off: 11:15am (8:15pm local)

Friday 15th October 2027

New Zealand v Hong Kong Docklands Stadium, Melbourne Kick-off: 7:15am (5:15pm local)

Saturday 16th October 2027

Australia v Chile Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 6:10am (3:10pm local)

Pool B

Sunday 3rd October 2027

South Africa v Italy Adelaide Oval, Adelaide Kick-off: 4:45am (2:15pm local)
Georgia v Romania North Queensland Stadium, Townsville Kick-off: 11:15am (8:15pm local)

Sunday 10th October 2027

South Africa v Georgia Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 7:45am (4:45pm local)

Monday 11th October 2027

Italy v Romania Sydney Football Stadium, Sydney Kick-off: 9:45am (7:45pm local)

Sunday 17th October 2027

Italy v Georgia Newcastle Stadium, Newcastle Kick-off: 2:15am (12:15pm local)
South Africa v Romania Perth Stadium, Perth Kick-off: 12:15pm (7:15pm local)

Pool C

Monday 4th October 2027

Fiji v Spain Newcastle Stadium, Newcastle Kick-off: 4:15am (2:15pm local)
Argentina v Canada Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 9:45am (6:45pm local)

Sunday 10th October 2027

Fiji v Canada Adelaide Oval, Adelaide Kick-off: 2:45am (12:15pm local)
Argentina v Spain Docklands Stadium, Melbourne Kick-off: 5:15am (3:15pm local)

Saturday 16th October 2027

Argentina v Fiji Adelaide Oval, Adelaide Kick-off: 3:45am (1:15pm local)
Spain v Canada North Queensland Stadium, Townsville Kick-off: 11:15am (8:15pm local)

Pool D

Sunday 3rd October 2027

Scotland v Uruguay Docklands Stadium, Melbourne Kick-off: 7:15am (5:15pm local)

Monday 4th October 2027

Ireland v Portugal Sydney Football Stadium, Sydney Kick-off: 7:15am (5:15pm local)

Sunday 10th October 2027

Ireland v Scotland Perth Stadium, Perth Kick-off: 10:45am (5:45pm local)

Monday 11th October 2027

Uruguay v Portugal Newcastle Stadium, Newcastle Kick-off: 7:15am (5:15pm local)

Sunday 17th October 2027

Ireland v Uruguay Docklands Stadium, Melbourne Kick-off: 4:45am (2:45pm local)
Scotland v Portugal Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 7:15am (4:15pm local)

Pool E

Saturday 2nd October 2027

France v USA Docklands Stadium, Melbourne Kick-off: 8:45am (5:45pm local)

Sunday 3rd October 2027

Japan v Samoa Newcastle Stadium, Newcastle Kick-off: 2:15am (12:15pm local)

Saturday 9th October 2027

USA v Samoa Perth Stadium, Perth Kick-off: 5:15am (12:15pm local)
France v Japan Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 9:45am (6:45pm local)

Friday 15th October 2027

Japan v USA Adelaide Oval, Adelaide Kick-off: 10:30am (8:00pm local)

Sunday 17th October 2027

France v Samoa Sydney Football Stadium, Sydney Kick-off: 9:45am (7:45pm local)

Pool F

Saturday 2nd October 2027

Wales v Zimbabwe Adelaide Oval, Adelaide Kick-off: 3:45am (12:15pm local)
England v Tonga Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 11:15am (8:15pm local)

Friday 8th October 2027

Wales v Tonga Docklands Stadium, Melbourne Kick-off: 8:15am (6:15pm local)
England v Zimbabwe Adelaide Oval, Adelaide Kick-off: 10:45am (8:15pm local)

Friday 15th October 2027

Tonga v Zimbabwe North Queensland Stadium, Townsville Kick-off: 11:15am (8:15pm local)

Saturday 16th October 2027

England v Wales Stadium Australia, Sydney Kick-off: 9:45am (7:45pm local)

Round of 16

Saturday 23rd October 2027

Runner-up Pool C v Runner-up Pool F Sydney Football Stadium, Sydney Kick-off: 4:15am (2:15pm local)
Winner Pool A v Best 3rd Pool C/E/F Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 6:45am (3:45pm local)
Winner Pool E v Runner-up Pool D Docklands Stadium, Melbourne Kick-off: 9:15am (7:15pm local)
Winner Pool B v Best 3rd Pool D/E/F Perth Stadium, Perth Kick-off: 11:45am (6:45pm local)

Sunday 24th October 2027

Winner Pool C v Best 3rd Pool A/E/F Sydney Football Stadium, Sydney Kick-off: 4:15am (2:15pm local)
Winner Pool D v Best 3rd Pool B/E/F Docklands Stadium, Melbourne Kick-off: 6:45am (4:45pm local)
Runner-up Pool A v Runner-up Pool E Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 9:15am (6:15pm local)
Winner Pool F v Runner-up Pool B Perth Stadium, Perth Kick-off: 11:45am (6:45pm local)

Quarter Finals

Saturday 30th October 2027

Winner R16 2 v Winner R16 4 Stadium Australia, Sydney Kick-off: 6:45am (4:45pm local)
Winner R16 1 v Winner R16 3 Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 9:45am (6:45pm local)

Sunday 31st October 2027

Winner R16 5 v Winner R16 6 Brisbane Stadium, Brisbane Kick-off: 6:00am (4:00pm local)
Winner R16 7 v Winner R16 8 Stadium Australia, Sydney Kick-off: 9:00am (8:00pm local)

Semi Finals

Friday 5th November 2027

Winner QF1 v Winner QF2 Stadium Australia, Sydney Kick-off: 9:00am (8:00pm local)

Saturday 6th November 2027

Winner QF3 v Winner QF4 Stadium Australia, Sydney Kick-off: 9:00am (8:00pm local)

Bronze Final

Friday 12th November 2027

Loser SF1 v Loser SF2 Stadium Australia, Sydney Kick-off: 8:45am (7:45pm local)

Rugby World Cup Final

Saturday 13th November 2027

Winner SF1 v Winner SF2 Stadium Australia, Sydney Kick-off: 9:00am (8:00pm local)

See also the official Rugby World Cup website.

Subscribe for news and updates

Rugby World Cup News

RFU backs Steve Borthwick to lead England into 2027 World Cup after review

5th May 2026

Steve Borthwick has been backed by the Rugby Football Union to lead England into next year’s World Cup following an investigation into the recent Six Nations failure.

Australia to open 2027 Rugby World Cup against Hong Kong

3rd February 2026

Australia will kick start the 2027 Rugby World Cup with an opener against Hong Kong in Perth.

Gregor Townsend wants Scotland to get win over Ireland before World Cup showdown

3rd December 2025

Gregor Townsend has challenged Scotland to banish their Ireland hoodoo before their showdown at the 2027 World Cup.

England to face Wales in group stage at 2027 Men's Rugby World Cup

3rd December 2025

England and Wales will clash in the group stage of the 2027 Men's Rugby World Cup.

Sydney's Olympic Stadium to host 2027 Rugby World Cup final

30th January 2025

Sydney’s Olympic Stadium will host the 2027 Rugby World Cup final, it has been announced.
